data.content: The file is generated successfully but never added to the `Compile` item group, so the compiler doesn't see it. The fix is to include the generated file in `Compile` (and `FileWrites` for clean support) within the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear: the `GenerateSampleCode` target generates a `.cs` file during the build, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The file is written to disk but MSBuild doesn't know to compile it.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group and to `FileWrites` (for clean support) within the target. Let me use the skill for this.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. The problem was that the `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never told MSBuild to compile it. The fix adds an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target that:

1. **`<Compile Include="...">`** — includes the generated file in compilation
2. **`<FileWrites Include="...">`** — ensures `dotnet clean` removes it
